description: Each eval gives a SwiftUI snippet with a tvOS deployment target. Agent must list tvOS rule IDs (e.g. tvOS-F01) violated, or confirm clean if none. Expected output names the violation set plus severity. False positives (e.g. flagging `.viewAligned` when none is present) are failures.

evals.name: container-focusable
evals.prompt: Review fixtures/container-focusable.swift for tvOS rule violations. Target: tvOS 17+. List every apple-tvos rule ID violated, or state 'clean' if none. Save verdict + rationale to ./verdict.md.
evals.expected_output: verdict.md cites tvOS-F01 (`.focusable()` on HStack container blocks focus reaching child CardView instances; remove `.focusable()` from the HStack — focus reaches cards via their `.focused($focusedID, equals:)`). No other violations.

evals.name: destructive-dialog-wrong-order
evals.prompt: Review fixtures/destructive-dialog.swift for tvOS rule violations. Target: tvOS 17+. List every apple-tvos rule ID violated, or state 'clean' if none. Save verdict + rationale to ./verdict.md.
evals.expected_output: verdict.md cites tvOS-A02 severity-1: destructive `Delete All` button declared first → default focus on Delete on tvOS, one Select press away from data loss. Fix: declare `Cancel` first.

evals.name: manual-focus-reassertion-and-sheet
evals.prompt: Review fixtures/manual-focus-reassertion.swift for tvOS rule violations. Target: tvOS 17+. List every apple-tvos rule ID violated, or state 'clean' if none. Save verdict + rationale to ./verdict.md.
evals.expected_output: verdict.md cites tvOS-A03 (manual focus reassertion via `DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter` hijacks VoiceOver/Switch Control; replace with `.defaultFocus` or scoped `@FocusState` on appear) AND tvOS-D01 (`.sheet()` for tvOS modal — focus containment not reliable; use `.fullScreenCover()`). Should also flag tvOS-A01 implicitly because the modal's `Close` button is visible without `#if !os(tvOS)` gating and lacks `.onExitCommand`.

evals.name: plain-button-with-custom-styling
evals.prompt: Review fixtures/plain-button-style.swift for tvOS rule violations. Target: tvOS 17+. List every apple-tvos rule ID violated, or state 'clean' if none. Save verdict + rationale to ./verdict.md.
evals.expected_output: verdict.md cites tvOS-D03: focusable Button uses `.buttonStyle(.plain)` with custom material background inside a horizontal ScrollView. Focus ring will clip against ScrollView. Fix: use `.buttonStyle(.bordered)` or `.buttonStyle(.card)` to let the focus engine own ring rendering.

evals.name: text-only-scrollview
evals.prompt: Review fixtures/text-only-scrollview.swift for tvOS rule violations. Target: tvOS 17+. List every apple-tvos rule ID violated, or state 'clean' if none. Save verdict + rationale to ./verdict.md.
evals.expected_output: verdict.md cites tvOS-F05: ScrollView contains only non-focusable Text views, so scrolling is impossible on tvOS (no focus target to drive scroll). Fix: either make each line `.focusable()` + `.focused($focusedID, equals: line.id)`, or branch with `#if os(tvOS)` to a paginated focusable variant.

evals.name: onexitcommand-correct-clean
evals.prompt: Review fixtures/onexitcommand-correct.swift for tvOS rule violations. Target: tvOS 17+. List every apple-tvos rule ID violated, or state 'clean' if none. Save verdict + rationale to ./verdict.md.
evals.expected_output: verdict.md states 'clean'. Snippet uses `.fullScreenCover()` (tvOS-D01 satisfied), Menu-button dismissal via `.onExitCommand` (tvOS-A01 satisfied), default focus set on appear via `@FocusState` without `DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter` (tvOS-A03 satisfied). No buttons use `.plain` with custom styling. No glass-on-glass. No destructive dialog. No issues.

evals.name: pod-without-focusstate
evals.prompt: Review fixtures/pod-without-focusstate.swift for tvOS rule violations. Target: tvOS 17+. List every apple-tvos rule ID violated, or state 'clean' if none. Save verdict + rationale to ./verdict.md.
evals.expected_output: verdict.md cites tvOS-F02: ChannelTile is POD (no @State / @Observable) and is made focusable but has no parent @FocusState + `.focused(_:equals:)` to anchor focus identity. On parent redraw of ChannelGrid, focus may move unexpectedly. Fix: add `@FocusState private var focusedID: Channel.ID?` and `.focused($focusedID, equals: channel.id)` on each tile.

evals.name: animation-on-focusable
evals.prompt: Review fixtures/animation-on-focusable.swift for tvOS rule violations. Target: tvOS 17+. List every apple-tvos rule ID violated, or state 'clean' if none. Save verdict + rationale to ./verdict.md.
evals.expected_output: verdict.md cites tvOS-F03: `.animation(.spring..., value: highlighted)` is applied to the Button (the focusable element) where it fights the built-in focus hover effect, producing jitter on real hardware. Fix: move `.animation(...)` inside the label closure scoped to the `.scaleEffect` child content, not on the outer Button.

evals.name: no-settle-delay
evals.prompt: Review fixtures/no-settle-delay.swift for tvOS rule violations. Target: tvOS 17+. List every apple-tvos rule ID violated, or state 'clean' if none. Save verdict + rationale to ./verdict.md.
evals.expected_output: verdict.md cites tvOS-F04: ShowCard starts an expensive video preview the moment focus arrives. Rapid swiping through cards starts and immediately cancels playback on every passed card. Fix: add a token-based settle delay (focusToken counter + Task.sleep guard) so only the final focused card triggers preview playback.

evals.name: paging-scroll
evals.prompt: Review fixtures/paging-scroll.swift for tvOS rule violations. Target: tvOS 17+. List every apple-tvos rule ID violated, or state 'clean' if none. Save verdict + rationale to ./verdict.md.
evals.expected_output: verdict.md cites tvOS-F06 (severity 3): `.scrollTargetBehavior(.paging)` on horizontal carousel produces jarring full-screen page jumps on 1920x1080. Fix: switch to `.scrollTargetBehavior(.viewAligned)` for smoother focus-to-focus navigation matching tvOS platform expectations. Bypass note: keep `.paging` only if the design intent is a true full-screen pager (onboarding, slideshow).

evals.name: focus-helper-traits
evals.prompt: Review fixtures/focus-helper-traits.swift for tvOS rule violations. Target: tvOS 17+. List every apple-tvos rule ID violated, or state 'clean' if none. Save verdict + rationale to ./verdict.md.
evals.expected_output: verdict.md cites tvOS-A04: zero-size `Rectangle().fill(.clear)` used purely as a default-focus helper carries `.accessibilityAddTraits(.isButton)` — VoiceOver announces a spurious 'Button' with no label. Fix: replace `.accessibilityAddTraits(.isButton)` with `.accessibilityHidden(true)`; better still, use `.defaultFocus($defaultFocus, .button)` on a real focusable element instead of a hidden Rectangle.

evals.name: glass-on-glass
evals.prompt: Review fixtures/glass-on-glass.swift for tvOS rule violations. Target: tvOS 17+. List every apple-tvos rule ID violated, or state 'clean' if none. Save verdict + rationale to ./verdict.md.
evals.expected_output: verdict.md cites tvOS-D02: `.buttonStyle(.glass)` and `.buttonStyle(.glassProminent)` applied to toolbar buttons. The system already renders toolbar chrome with Liquid Glass treatment on tvOS — stacking glass-style buttons on top produces muddy double-blur. Fix: drop the explicit glass button styles in chrome; the system applies the appropriate treatment automatically. Reserve `.glass` / `.glassProminent` for non-chrome contexts.
